subject: Geography
title: Iceland: Fire and Ice
description: Learn Iceland: Fire and Ice in one minute.

hook.text: Iceland sits on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge, where two tectonic plates pull apart.

buildup.text: The island is half glaciers, half volcanoes—earning its 'fire and ice' nickname.

discovery.text: In 2010, Eyjafjallajökull erupted and grounded European air travel for weeks.

twist.text: Iceland heats 90% of its homes with geothermal energy tapped from volcanic rock.

climax.text: The island grows about 2.5 cm wider each year as the plates pull apart.

punchline.text: An island literally tearing itself apart—and growing.

quiz.question: How does Iceland heat most of its homes?
